title: 10 best herbal teas for fever
intro_desc: Herbal teas for fever are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as body aches, headaches, and restlessness. These teas work by stimulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and promoting sweating, which helps to bring down body temperature. Examples of herbal teas that help with fever include peppermint, ginger, and yarrow tea, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Drinking these teas can significantly improve lives by providing relief from discomfort, allowing individuals to rest and recover faster, and reducing the risk of complications associated with high fevers.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic compounds: Kansas coneflower tea contains antipyretic compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing prostaglandin synthesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its rich content of polyphenols and terpenes, help to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as pain, swelling, and redness.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower tea has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of febrile illnesses by modulating the activity of cytokines and other immune cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe and calm the body during a fever.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain saponins, which have antipyretic properties, making them effective at reducing fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried echinacea angustifolia root and add it to a heat-resistant teapot.
Boil one cup of filtered water in the teapot for five minutes then turn off the heat immediately.
Steep the dried echinacea angustifolia root in the boiling water for seven to ten minutes to infuse the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the warm herbal tea within one hour of preparation as needed up to three times a day.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with fever because it contains a unique combination of compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help to reduce the severity of fever symptoms by suppressing the release of prostaglandins, a type of hormone that causes inflammation. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Kansas coneflower can help to combat infections that may be contributing to the fever, ultimately promoting a faster recovery.

Maidenhair tree teas helps with fever because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and modulate the body's response to infection. The tea's active ingredients, such as maidenhair extract and salicin, have natural analgesic and anti-pyretic properties, which help to ease discomfort, relieve pain, and lower body temperature, providing relief from fever-related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic: The flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tea have been shown to exhibit antipyretic properties, which means they can help reduce fever by blocking the production of prostaglandins, a key molecule responsible for triggering the body's thermoregulatory response.

Anti-inflammatory: The terpenes and volatile oils found in Maidenhair Tree tea possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headaches, muscle pains, and joint stiffness by reducing inflammation in the body.

Immune-stimulating: The saponins present in Maidenhair Tree tea have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells that help fight off infections, which can lead to fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease body aches associated with fever.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate the discomfort caused by fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves according to local regulations and guidelines.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Boil one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in four cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl or container.
Allow the tea to cool and steep for an additional 30-45 minutes before serving and consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with fever because of its natural cooling properties that help to reduce body temperature. The menthol present in peppermint soothes a sore throat, relieves congestion, and calms a feverish mind and body. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds ease muscle aches and pains associated with fever, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness. As the tea's refreshing flavor and aroma work to revive and rejuvenate, it helps to alleviate the discomforts of fever, making it an effective and soothing remedy for those suffering from a high temperature.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, has natural analgesic and antipyretic properties that help reduce fever by dilating blood vessels and improving circulation, allowing the body to cool itself more efficiently.

Antioxidant compounds: Peppermint tea contains a range of antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by fever, reducing its severity and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, can help reduce the body's natural response to infection, leading to a decrease in swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are rich in menthol, which helps to cool and soothe the body, reducing fever symptoms.
Barks: The barks of peppermint contain salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever that can help to reduce fever and alleviate headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon dried peppermint leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the peppermint leaves in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the tea hot, ideally within 15-20 minutes of preparation, to help alleviate symptoms of fever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with fever because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of a fever. The flavonoids and antioxidants present in elder tea have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria, thereby helping to combat infection and alleviate symptoms such as headache, body ache, and fatigue. Additionally, elder tea's warming properties can help to relax the body and promote sweating, which is an effective way to bring down a fever naturally.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder tea contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which help to combat viral infections that cause fever by inhibiting the replication of viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and saponins, which reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headache, muscle aches, and joint pain.

Mucolytic activity: Elder tea contains mucolytic agents that help to break down mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections like the common cold and flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with it.
Barks: Contains tannins that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against fever-related infections and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers and measure out one teaspoon.
Combine the measured sambucus nigra ingredients with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal properties of the herbs.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately to help alleviate fever symptoms within 15-30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lavender tea have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, effectively combating the underlying causes of fever. Additionally, the calming and soothing nature of lavender can help alleviate discomfort and restlessness associated with fever, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lavender's antiviral compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce the severity and duration of viral infections, including fevers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms like headaches and body aches.

Sedative properties: Lavender's calming effects, thanks to its sesquiterpenes and linalool content, can help to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate related symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which provide a calming effect and help to soothe a feverish body.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers per cup of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water to a boil and then pour it over the dried lavender flowers in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on personal preference for strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender solids.
Serve the tea hot, as needed, taking one to two cups per day to help alleviate fever symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with fever because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce body temperature by increasing sweat production, which in turn cools down the body. The active compound gingerol also has antiviral and antibacterial properties that help to combat infection-causing pathogens, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms associated with fever. Additionally, ginger teas have been shown to relax the throat muscles, alleviating soreness and discomfort often experienced during a fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like headaches, sore throats, and body aches associated with fever.
Antiviral properties: The antiviral properties of ginger tea may help combat the underlying viral infection causing the fever, reducing its severity and duration.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, also known as the underground stem, has natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in reducing fever and soothing body aches.
Roots: Ginger roots have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, due to their ability to stimulate sweating and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginger root and two cups of boiling water.
Steep the ginger root in the boiling water for seven minutes and thirty seconds.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste and stir well, with an option to add lemon juice if desired.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat every two hours as needed until fever subsides.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm teas helps with fever because of its unique mucilaginous properties. When consumed as a warm tea, slippery elm's gel-like coating soothes the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and chest. This helps to ease the discomfort associated with fevers, making it easier for the body to expel toxins and recover from illness. Additionally, slippery elm's demulcent properties may also help to reduce fever by providing a cooling sensation that calms the body's temperature-regulating mechanisms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and joint pain associated with fever.
Mucilages: The mucilage content in slippery elm tea helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by fever-related congestion and coughing.

Antiviral properties: Slippery elm's antiviral compounds may help prevent or combat viral infections that cause fever, such as the common cold or flu, by inhibiting the replication of viruses in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making it an effective remedy for relieving fever-related congestion.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of salicin, similar to aspirin, which helps reduce fever and alleviate headache and body aches associated with illness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried ulmus rubra bark or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the harvested material with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavonoids and phenolic acids to infuse.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the cooled tea every 2 hours as needed for fever relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with fever because it contains flavonoids and terpenes that have natural anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds help to reduce the body's internal temperature by increasing sweating and promoting blood flow to the skin. This can provide relief from fever-related discomforts such as headaches, fatigue, and chills. Additionally, yarrow teas' antibacterial and antiviral properties may also help to combat underlying infections that contribute to fever.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Yarrow tea has been shown to have antipyretic effects, which means it can help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headache and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in yarrow tea, such as sesquiterpene lactones, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain caused by infection or inflammation, making it an effective remedy for fever-related conditions like sinusitis and bronchitis.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tea is rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fever. This can also help mitigate potential damage to tissues and organs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Rhizomes: Contain a high concentration of volatile oils that help to relax blood vessels and lower body temperature, making it suitable for treating fevers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris before drying.
Dry the cleaned achillea millefolium leaves and stems using a dehydrator set at 60°c for 2 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a soothing tea infusion.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and let it cool slightly before drinking within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with fever because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including eucalyptol and cineole. These natural antiseptics and decongestants help to reduce inflammation in the body, relieving congestion and opening up airways to make breathing easier. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing the severity of coughs and fever symptoms. Its soothing and calming effects also promote a restful night's sleep, aiding the body in its natural recovery process.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol, which can help relieve respiratory congestion and reduce inflammation in the body.

Antimicrobial Activity: The essential oils in Eucalyptus tea have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections that contribute to fever development and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and sneezing.

Expectorant Properties: The mucolytic properties of Eucalyptus oil can help break down mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs, making it easier for the body to clear out excess phlegm and reduce congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are excellent for soothing fevers and reducing body temperature.
Barks: The rough, outer layer of the tree's trunk contains salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and antipyretic agent.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle and pour it over the dried eucalyptus leaves in a large cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for five minutes, releasing the medicinal properties of the eucalyptus into the tea.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Drink the warm tea immediately and repeat every two hours as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The tea's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it an effective remedy for soothing a sore throat and alleviating symptoms associated with colds and flu. Additionally, lemongrass tea has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to calm the digestive system and promote sweating, further aiding in the elimination of toxins and reducing fever.
remedy_properties: Citral: Citral, a compound found in lemongrass, has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in lemonggrass tea, such as citronellal and geraniol, have a cooling effect that can help bring down body temperature and provide relief from fever-related discomforts.
Antioxidants: Lemongrass tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemongrass roots help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of volatile oils, lemongrass rhyzomes possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe aching muscles and joints during fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and chop them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped cymbopogon citratus with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the active compounds and flavonoids from the leaves.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Serve the cymbopogon citratus tea hot, with honey if desired, and drink immediately to alleviate fever symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat fever with herbal teas is to brew a soothing blend of calming herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and lemon balm. These herbs help to reduce body temperature, ease headache and body pain, and promote relaxation. Simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as needed. Repeat every few hours or as required.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fever that are treated with herbal teas are headaches, migraines, and body aches. Teas made from herbs such as willow bark, ginger, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and relieve discomfort. Other herbs like Echinacea and Goldenseal have immune-boosting properties that can aid in the recovery process. These teas can provide natural relief without the harsh side effects of over-the-counter medications.
